description: Streamlit UI for presenting the drafted strategy and JSON diffs to the user, allowing them to approve or provide feedback to resume the LangGraph workflow.

userStories.title: Display Drafted Strategies and Diffs
userStories.description: As a user, I want to review the drafted strategy and resume diffs for jobs that are in the STRATEGY_DRAFTED state, so that I can decide whether the AI's proposed changes meet my standards.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the Streamlit dashboard is loaded
and Given there is at least one job with status STRATEGY_DRAFTED
When I look at the Action Required sections
Then I see a section for reviewing drafted strategies
and Then I can select a job and view its strategy_markdown and resume_diffs from the LangGraph state

tasks.description: Update app.py to filter jobs with status STRATEGY_DRAFTED and create a selectbox to choose one.
Extract strategy_markdown and resume_diffs from the SqliteSaver checkpointer for the selected job.
Render the strategy_markdown using st.markdown and display the resume_diffs clearly (e.g., using st.json or a custom representation).

userStories.title: Approve or Provide Feedback on Strategies
userStories.description: As a user, I want to approve or provide feedback on the drafted strategy, so that the LangGraph workflow can resume with my approval or revision requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Given I am reviewing a drafted strategy for a job
Then the graph state is updated with empty user_feedback and execution resumes
and Given I am reviewing a drafted strategy for a job
When I enter text into the feedback field and submit
Then the graph state is updated with my user_feedback and execution resumes

tasks.description: Add an approval button and a text area for feedback in app.py for the selected job strategy.
Implement logic for the 'Approve' button to inject user_feedback='' (or similar null signal) into the child_graph state and resume it via graph.stream.
Implement logic for the feedback submission to inject the text area content as user_feedback into the child_graph state and resume it.
